Patch Set 2: Code-Review-1

(3 comments)

Two things that I think are wrong with this patch, since I don't think it is actually solving the problem that is reported in LU-11703.  It may be solving _some_ issue, but I'm not sure it is the right one:
- the mmap overwrite of the file should not be consuming more space or grant on the client, since the same blocks are repeatedly being overwritten.  This indicates that there is actually some bug in the grant consumption on the client.
- the client _may_ still run out of grant if the filesystem is actually full, so SIGBUS should not happen if the file is already preallocated.  At worst the client should do a sync write in this case, but I suspect if the grant consumption issue is fixed then this won't be a problem.  Under normal circumstances, the client might consume grant, but it should be returned to the client immediately if the blocks are already allocated.

Patch Set 2:

I agree with Andreas - I don't see how/why this is consuming grant space beyond the original amount.

I also think I don't understand why the OBD_BRW_FROM_GRANT check is removed.  Basically - I think - if there are client pages which did not consume grant, then we do the sync.  Otherwise we do not.

Why is this check incorrect?  If it's because OBD_BRW_FROM_GRANT is set (or not set) incorrectly, shouldn't we fix that?

It's probably fine to always do the sync, and the change to the "left" check seems OK(?), but it doesn't seem necessary either.

Patch Set 2:

> (3 comments)
 > 
 > Two things that I think are wrong with this patch, since I don't
 > think it is actually solving the problem that is reported in
 > LU-11703.  It may be solving _some_ issue, but I'm not sure it is
 > the right one:
 > - the mmap overwrite of the file should not be consuming more space
 > or grant on the client, since the same blocks are repeatedly being
 > overwritten.  This indicates that there is actually some bug in the
 > grant consumption on the client.

During this test async i/o gets unplugged in case when number of subsequent dirty pages reaches max_pages_per_rpc which is 1024 by default. Once async i/o is unplugged, dirty pages become clean in (osc_extent_make_ready->osc_make_ready->vvp_page_make_ready->clear_page_dirty_for_io). Then ll_page_mkwrite() consumes grants for those pages even in case of overwrite.

 > - the client _may_ still run out of grant if the filesystem is
 > actually full, so SIGBUS should not happen if the file is already
 > preallocated.  At worst the client should do a sync write in this
 > case, but I suspect if the grant consumption issue is fixed then
 > this won't be a problem.  Under normal circumstances, the client
 > might consume grant, but it should be returned to the client
 > immediately if the blocks are already allocated.

I will check that case.

> This is filling the whole filesystem with 12MB mmap writes?  That would take a long time...

ok, the test is changed to address this defect.

> Hmm, but it is rewriting the same file over and over again, so it shouldn't be consuming any more space on the server.

As said above - submitted pages become not dirty, so consumed space grows on overwrite if pages gets submitted.

Patch Set 2:

> I agree with Andreas - I don't see how/why this is consuming grant
 > space beyond the original amount.
 > 

Submitted pages become not dirty, so consumed space grows on overwrite.

 > I also think I don't understand why the OBD_BRW_FROM_GRANT check is
 > removed.  Basically - I think - if there are client pages which did
 > not consume grant, then we do the sync.  Otherwise we do not.
 > 
 > Why is this check incorrect? 

In case of mmaped i/o a client has no possibility to switch to synchronous commit in case of grant lack, as a process which is doing mmaped i/o  gets SIGBUS if page fault fails. However, pages submitted via mmaped i/o have OBD_BRW_FROM_GRANT set and it is worth to sync those pages as well when free space is over.

> If it's because OBD_BRW_FROM_GRANT is
 > set (or not set) incorrectly, shouldn't we fix that?
 > 
 > It's probably fine to always do the sync,

yes

>  and the change to the
 > "left" check seems OK(?), but it doesn't seem necessary either.

current the “left” check is fine, there is no need to modify it.

Patch Set 3:

Strictly speaking, it shouldn't be necessary to sync mmap writes, because mmap file space should always be allocated to avoid ENOSPC causing SIGBUS.  That is why mmap writes always use FROM_GRANT and are supposed to avoid running out of grants.

Patch Set 3:

> Strictly speaking, it shouldn't be necessary to sync mmap writes,
 > because mmap file space should always be allocated to avoid ENOSPC
 > causing SIGBUS.  That is why mmap writes always use FROM_GRANT and
 > are supposed to avoid running out of grants.

The problem exists even for files which already have allocated space.

The following tests were performed:
1. overwrite mmap I/O to 8mb long file with allocated space
 results:
- without this patch: SIGBUS
- with the patch: ok

2. sequential mmaped I/O to a file which does not have allocated space yet until disk space is over:
result:
- without this patch: SIGBUG, no blocks get written
- with the patch: number of blocks written is close to what buffered write (dd) achieves.

Patch Set 3:

Looks good to me.

Patch Set 3: Code-Review+2

Oleg corrected me on the requirement for files to be preallocated for mmap().  sorry for the long delay in response, you should ping on patches that are not getting attention.

Abandoned

This patch is not needed after
LU-13228 clio: mmap write when overquota:
vvp_io_commit_sync() called by vvp_io_fault_start() forces the required transaction commit.

commit.subject: LU-11703 target: force transaction commit on grant lack
commit.message:
LU-11703 target: force transaction commit on grant lack

The following test
    addr = mmap(.., MAP_SHARED, ..);
    for(;;)
        memcpy(addr, ...);

may end up with receiving SIGBUS. This is because ll_page_mkwrite()
(lustre implementation of page_mkwrite method of vm_operations_struct)
is unable to handle case of insufficient grants.

An OST allocates grants based on amount of space available on a
backend filesystem reduced by amount of space already granted to
clients (tgt_grant_space_left()). The amount of space granted to
clients gets released in transaction commit hook (tgt_grant_commit()).
If the OST manages to grant almost all available space to clients in
time interval between subsequent transaction commits - it degrades
grants allocation so that clients end up with ll_page_mkwrite failure.

The fix is to sync a target which is close to free space exhaustion
regardless to whether pages to be written back consume grant space or
not. This is because in case of mmaped i/o there is no way to switch
to sync write.

The case illustrating the issue is added to mmap_sanity test.

Signed-off-by: Vladimir Saveliev <c17830@cray.com>
Change-Id: I8c399edd655aaa9a566003325e0edc4b2be45839
Cray-bug-id: LUS-2146
